steps.assistant_text: 115-insn six-element list builder. The existing NM wrap was already 113/115 byte-exact (98.26% fuzzy): the prologue (dead a0 home + dead `or a3,a0,zero` via a0-as-dead-4th-arg), the +8 frame via `volatile int pad[2]` declared last, the s-reg cascade, the per-element back-link, and all relocs were exact. The sole residual was a 2-insn as1-scheduler tie after the first element's call: the target schedules `sw list,0x28(sp)` (home the list stack temp) BEFORE `or s0,v0` (save r to callee-saved s0); the default statement layout picked the opposite tie order. The permuter cracked it by COLLAPSING the first element's three statements (`r = func_00000000(0,a1,...)`, `list = s1 + 0x10`, `func_00000000(list, r)`) onto a SINGLE source line. IDO's as1 scheduler uses the per-statement debug line number as a tie-break, so sharing one line re-anchors the tie to emit the list-home store first, matching the target. Whitespace-only steer (the C is semantically identical); the same-line grouping is load-bearing.
